articles.title: Gold rebounds from multi‑month low; bullish USD and Fed hike bets to cap upside
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) stages a modest recovery from the $4,480 region, or its lowest level since March 30, touched during the Asian session on Monday, though the upside potential seems limited. The US Dollar (USD) buying remains unabated in the wake of persistent geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.title: BoE's Breeden says political uncertainty is hitting the business environment — FT
articles.expand: Bank of England (BoE) Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden warned that political uncertainty is hitting the business environment and cautioned that the UK central bank should not be "trigger happy" when adjusting interest rates, the Financial Times reported on Monday.

articles.title: BOE policymaker Breeden says no need to rush in taking next policy step
articles.expand: We do not need to rush We are in a good place to be able to watch what’s happening in the economy There is time to understand the size of the shocks and also how the economy is evolving Should not be trigger-happy on rates Cannot wait forever but that doesn't mean we need to react in June or July Her comments are nothing that really jumps out of th...

articles.title: Swiss Franc flatlines near multi-week low on Fed hike repricing
articles.expand: The USD/CHF pair holds steady near 0.7870 during the early European session on Monday. The pair currently trades near the highest since April 30, bolstered by a stronger US Dollar (USD). Traders will closely monitor the developments surrounding the US-Iran conflicts.

articles.title: AUD/JPY Price Forecast: Tests ascending triangle bottom near 113.00
articles.expand: AUD/JPY extends its losses for the third successive day, trading around 113.20 during the Asian hours on Monday. The technical analysis of the daily chart suggests a potential busted pattern or bearish failure as the currency cross is positioned on the lower trendline of an ascending triangle.

articles.title: Chart Art: AUD/CAD’s Pullback to a Major Area of Interest
articles.expand: Looks like AUD/CAD is pulling back after making fresh 2026 highs! How low can the pair go before the bulls step back in?

articles.title: US Dollar Index refreshes five-week high near 99.40 as oil prices extend advance
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback’s value against six major currencies, starts the week on a positive note, as oil prices extend its advance due to fears that the war between the United States (US) and Iran will resume.

articles.title: Event Guide: Canada’s CPI Report (April 2026)
articles.expand: Canada’s CPI report could decide whether the Loonie shrugs off higher energy prices or starts pricing in broader inflation trouble.

articles.title: China April data misses badly, Iran war and weak demand weigh. Retail sales growth plunge.
articles.expand: China's April retail sales rose just 0.2%, the weakest since 2022, while industrial output grew only 4.1%, badly missing forecasts, as the Iran war and sluggish domestic demand weighed. Summary: China's National Bureau of Statistics reported April industrial output growth of 4.1% year-on-year, down from 5.7% in March and well below the 5.9% forecas...

articles.title: Australian Dollar remains subdued below 0.7150 following China’s data
articles.expand: AUD/USD loses ground for the third consecutive day, trading around 0.7130 during the Asian hours on Monday. The pair depreciates following key economic data from Australia’s close trading partner, China.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar hangs near one-month low vs bullish USD; rising Oil prices limit losses
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair trades with a positive bias above mid-1.3700s during the Asian session on Monday, though it remains below a one-month top touched last Friday.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar holds losses below 0.5850 on weak Chinese data
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair trades in negative territory around 0.5830 during the Asian trading hours on Monday. The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) faces some selling pressure following the downbeat Chinese economic data.

articles.title: Japan's 10-year bond yield hits 1996 high as fresh debt plans emerge. Extra budget coming
articles.expand: Japan's 10-year bond yield hit its highest since 1996 at 4.2% after a government source confirmed fresh debt issuance is planned to fund a supplementary budget to offset Iran war energy costs. Summary: A Japanese government source told Reuters that fresh debt issuance will likely form part of the funding for a planned supplementary budget aimed at ...

articles.title: Asia open: Trump-Xi summit disappoints, inflation fears fuel bond yield surge
articles.expand: Global markets opened cautiously after the Trump-Xi Beijing summit ended with limited progress on trade and geopolitical tensions. Rising inflation fears and surging bond yields intensified concerns that the Federal Reserve may eventually consider rate hikes rather than cuts. Investors are increasingly worried about extreme concentration within the...

articles.title: Japanese Yen weakens as hawkish Fed bets drive USD higher
articles.expand: USD/JPY extends its gains for the sixth successive day, trading around 158.90 during the Asian hours on Monday. The US Dollar (USD) gains value against other currencies because the US Federal Reserve (Fed) is shifting toward a more aggressive stance on inflation.

articles.title: PBOC is expected to set the USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8086 – Reuters estimate
articles.expand: The People’s Bank of China is due to set the daily USD/CNY reference rate at around 0115 GMT (2115 US Eastern time), a fixing that remains one of the most closely watched signals in Asian foreign exchange markets. articles.title: Gold declines below $4,550 on rising Fed hike expectations
articles.expand: Gold price (XAU/USD) declines to around $4,535 during the early Asian trading hours on Monday. The precious metal remains on the defensive as heightened inflation concerns, due to the conflict in the Middle East, reinforce bets for higher interest rates.

articles.title: Australian Dollar holds steady near 0.7150 amid rising Fed hike bets, Middle East tensions
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair trades on a flat note near 0.7150 during the early Asian session on Monday. The pair steadies after retreating from multi-year highs amid shifting US interest rate expectations and ongoing geopolitical tensions.

articles.expand: New Zealand's services sector contracted for a second straight month in April, with the PSI reading at 48.9, as businesses cited fuel prices and the Strait of Hormuz conflict as key headwinds. Summary: The BNZ-BusinessNZ PSI came in at 48.9 in April, up from 46.2 in March but still below the 50.0 expansion threshold, with BusinessNZ CEO Katherine R...

articles.title: Inflation data may let the RBNZ off the hook in May, but rate hikes loom
articles.expand: Barring any major surprises, recent data has taken a May rate hike off the table, but most economists agree that rate increases aren’t far off. The Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) is mandated to keep inflation between 1% and 3%.

articles.title: Australian economy expected to add thousands more jobs
articles.expand: The Australian Bureau of Statistics is forecast to post a positive gain in jobs when it publishes new data, as economists mull over sweeping tax changes.

articles.title: British Pound tumbles as UK turmoil, Iran risks lift USD
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair extends on Friday its losses for the fourth straight day, poised to finish the week down more than 2% as political turmoil in the UK and increased speculation that Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s successor could widen fiscal deficits weigh on the currency.

articles.expand: The price of silver is under intense pressure today, tumbling $6.55 or -7.84% to $76.90. The sharp decline marks the largest one-day percentage drop since March 3, when the metal fell -8.15%, and reflects a dramatic shift in momentum after silver once again failed near a major resistance target around the $90 level. articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: No more waiting despite uncertainty leads
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) surged the most in two weeks in mid-May, resulting in EUR/USD falling to 1.1617, its lowest in over a month. The pair held nearby as Friday came to an end, hinting at a downward continuation in the upcoming days.

articles.expand: USD/CHF remains supported by rising US yields and a wide interest rate differential, making dollar dips attractive despite the franc’s safe-haven status.

articles.title: EUR/USD Forecast: Euro Holds 1.17 Support as Traders Await US Jobs Report
articles.expand: EUR/USD is bouncing from 1.17 support but remains trapped in a tight range ahead of Friday’s US jobs report and ongoing bond-market volatility.
